clean_body: | | | Phil Scott's Week In Action | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | Greetings, | This was an exciting week filled with notable endorsements, Phil’s strong debate performances demonstrating the clear contrast in this election and, as always, incredible enthusiasm from supporters around the state. Check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign. EARLY VOTING STILL GOING STRONG Early voting is open now, so click to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot, and get those votes in for Phil now. We’re so encouraged by all of the early voting support we’re hearing about on social media. Let’s keep rolling straight through to a strong victory on November 8! Voted early for Phil? Share your support with us on Twitter and Facebook: and * PHIL’S ECONOMIC PLAN: PRO-JOBS TAX POLICIES This probably won’t come as a surprise to any of you: Vermont is a high tax state. Making Vermont more affordable will be one of Phil’s top priorities, and taxes are a big focus. Phil’s outlined several proposals to simplify tax laws, benchmark spending against specific outcomes and create a more welcoming and predictable environment for job creators. These proposals are part of his comprehensive economic development plan, which you can view . Phil will work to make state government more efficient and he will propose investing a portion of those savings in lowering the personal and business tax burden across the board. He’ll create a more welcoming environment for retirees and veterans by phasing out the tax on both social security and military retirement benefits. He’ll also work to restore the full Research & Development tax credit and the film production tax credit. He’ll explore a number of approaches to encourage investment of private capital and establish a real angel investor tax credit. A Phil Scott Administration will use the tax code strategically to stimulate economic growth. Read more about these proposals (beginning on page 21). Photo Credit: @PhilScottVT PHIL EARNS ENDORSEMENT OF MULTIPLE LAW ENFORCEMENT GROUPS Citing his leadership on law enforcement issues and commitment to public safety, both the Vermont Police Association and the Vermont Troopers Association this week endorsed Phil for Governor. “During Mr. Scott’s years of public service as Senator and Lt. Governor, he has demonstrated a true commitment to public safety legislation serving Vermont’s best interest. Lt. Governor Scott fully understands the challenges posed by the array of public safety issues Vermont must address. The Vermont Police Association believes that Mr. Scott can be relied upon to address those issues ,” said George P. Merkel, President of the Vermont Police Association. Read the full endorsement . “Lt. Governor Scott has said on many occasions that a critical role of government is ensuring public safety. We look forward to working with him in the future to improve fair and impartial law enforcement standards, combat the opiate epidemic, and address the array of issues Troopers face every day in Vermont ,” said Sgt. Michael O’Neil, President of the Vermont Troopers Association. Read the full endorsement . BIPARTISAN SUPPORT FOR PHIL CONTINUES TO GROW Throughout Phil’s entire career he has made a practice of listening to all perspectives and considering any idea that will help improve the lives of Vermonters, regardless of which party it comes from. He’s always willing to work across the aisle, and understands that we must work together in order to make real progress. That leadership style, along with his pro-growth plans and commitment to addressing our crisis of affordability, has attracted support from Vermonters of all political parties. That support is on full display in a new television ad highlighting some of the many Vermont Democrats who plan to vote for Phil on November 8. “Everywhere I go, in my Senate district and throughout Vermont, I hear from self-proclaimed Democrats that Phil Scott is the clear choice in this election for Governor ,” said Sen. Dick Mazza (D-Grand Isle) in the ad. “Most importantly, Phil will provide the balance we need in Montpelier to ensure we’re all focused on growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable so we can invest in our children, communities and future. He’ll be a Governor for every Vermonter, not just one side or the other,” Mazza concludes. Read the official press release and watch it below. | Phil Scott for Vermont: Democrats for Phil | *** AND DOWN THE STRETCH WE GO! We made the rounds again this week with stops across the state, giving Phil the opportunity to meet with so many great people, from students to seniors; from young professionals to seasoned industry veterans! Phil met with the Vermont Town & City Management Association in Stowe , the Vermont Community Development Association at Castleton , and Young Professionals in Rutland and Burlington. He also stopped into the Wayside in Montpelier , the 14 th Star Brewery in St. Albans, Fab-Tech in Colchester , attended the CVU Craft Fair , and met with residents in Chittenden and St. Albans. Taking some time to ensure Vermont’s school-age children get a lesson in the importance of public service, Phil visited students at Burr and Burton Academy and had a video conference with 1 st and 2nd graders from Waitsfield. Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events . ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS Connecting with students at Burr and Burton Academy “Scott's address focused on education as an economic engine for the state. He said he wants to invest in early childhood programs and reduce the cost of K-12 education while building flexibility into Act 46 to maintain or expand school choice. He said early childhood services need to be more widely available, more comprehensive and more affordable, while administrative costs for K-12 schools need to be reduced.” (Source: Bennington Banner: ) VPR poll shows Phil most trusted candidate on the economy and affordability “Scott — whose campaign has focused on making the state more affordable and tightening the budget — was seen as the more trustworthy candidate for voters with anxiety about the economy and cost of living. On the trustworthiness question, voters picked Scott over Minter 41 percent to 28 percent.” (Source: VT Digger: ) Phil on energy goals and affordability "What we should be doing is increasing our focus on becoming energy independent by 2050.” (Source: WCAX: ) Deerfield Valley News: Economy top priority for Phil “I believe we can do better, so (the economy) is going to be my focus. That’s what I think this election is all about—restoring faith and trust, solving the issue of affordability, and focusing on the economy so we have more prosperity for everyone.” (Source: Deerfield Valley News: ) Phil Scott is my man “He knows how to sit down at the table and negotiate with others so there is progress rather than impasse. He is realistic about our future and dedicated to allowing Vermonters to be freely independent, able to find a good job/business to support themselves and their families.” – Mary Daly, Chairwoman, Orange County Republican Committee (Source: Burlington Free Press: ) Support for Scott “Phil Scott has been our Lt. Governor for many years, and has the ability to work with both parties. He understands the needs of the people of Vermont and will work to resolve the problems we now face.” – Stephen McConnel, Townshend (Source: Brattleboro Reformer: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS A clear choice This one is a #GoodWatch, rather than a #GoodRead, but ICYMI, the WCAX debate this week illustrated the clear choice in this election : Phil is the only candidate who will make the economy and affordability priorities in Montpelier come January. And as Gov. Jim Douglas told Phil’s supporters after the debate, “Phil Scott distinguished himself as the clear leader. He was passionate. He was focused. He laid out a vision for the future - to get our economy back on track and grow good jobs. Most importantly, he was gubernatorial.” Watch the full debate . Stowe Reporter: Phil Scott wants to lead, inspire, get the best from people Phil sat down with the Stowe Reporter and talked leadership, working across the aisle and, of course, the economy! As Phil told the Reporter , he will work to make the state more predictable for businesses so that they can create more jobs, helping to raise wages. He’ll also work to retain and attract more young, working-age Vermonters with affordable housing options and explore greater incentives. Phil also talks about education spending and school choice, and his plan to address our opioid crisis. Read the full article , and read more about each of Phil’s plans to grow our economy, make Vermont more affordable, and address critical challenges . Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – and . Sincerely, Team Scott | "DONATE TODAY!" | "JOIN THE TEAM!" | | | | | | | | | | | | Phil Scott for Governor, Glen Wright, Treasurer, 46 Three Mile Bridge Road, Middlesex, VT 05602 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or

name: Jessica O'Connell, EMILY's List
subject: This moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62cd/2683929265/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all")
---

| | Hi there,

We say it every cycle, but it's never been more true -- this is the most crucial cycle in EMILY's List history.

This community has been through a lot -- from supporting the first Democratic woman elected to the Senate in her own right to the Year of the Woman in 1992 to reclaiming the House with the first woman speaker in 2006. But this is the first time we've had the chance to elect a woman to the presidency AND help decide who controls the Senate.

We've become a force in the Democratic Party and national politics because no matter how hard the fight is, we never back down. But to defeat Donald Trump AND flip the Senate, we're going to have to fight harder than ever over these next 18 days.

**[Be a part of this critical moment: Donate $3 to help flip the Senate and elect Hillary today.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62ce/2683929265/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20161021_email_31years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+if+consinConsGroup8472+Our+records+assoc&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all")**

Democrats need to pick up just five seats to flip the Senate, and we have six women running to flip Republican Senate seats. We also have a candidate in Nevada working to block Republicans from their only pick-up opportunity. If we give these candidates enough support, we can decide control of the Senate: The math is in our favor.

We also have to help elect Hillary in November. She's the most qualified candidate in history, and given her record of fighting for women and families, we know she's going to make an amazing president. But Donald Trump is not going to go down without a fight.

While the polls look good for Hillary now, there's truly no telling what will happen (or what he'll do) in the next 18 days.

EMILY's List's 31 years of helping candidates has taught us to never get complacent, count our chickens before they hatch, or be blindsided when the dynamic in a race changes right before Election Day.

**Donate $3 right now to help our women win during this critical election:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Its-Our-Moment](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62cf/2683929265/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20161021_email_31years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgItsOurMoment&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all")**

Thank you,

Jess O'Connell
Executive Director, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62c8/2683929265/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20161021_email_31years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all")

|
---|---|---
# EMILY's List is a community of over three million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office.

|

1800 M Street NW, Suite 375N, Washington, DC 20036 Thank you for being a supporter of EMILY's List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email [here](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62ca/2683929265/VEsA/ "http://action.emilyslist.org/page/s/less-email-private-bc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe [here](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b6d/24c69fd9/c76105d/18ea62cb/2683929265/VEsB/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all&source=WPEFEV59N_digital_email_20161021_31yrs_n-all"). Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at [information@emilyslist.org](mailto:information@emilyslist.org) or simply reply to this email.

EMILY's List's 31 years of helping candidates has taught us to never get complacent, count our chickens before they hatch, or be blindsided when the dynamic in a race changes right before Election Day. Donate $3 right now to help our women win during this critical election: "" Thank you, Jess O'Connell Executive Director, EMILY's List "" | ---|---|--- EMILY's List is a community of over three million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office. | Paid for by EMILY's List "" and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 1800 M Street NW, Suite 375N, Washington, DC 20036 Thank you for being a supporter of EMILY's List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email "". We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe "". Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILY's List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible.

body:
| | Friend -- I'll keep this short.

I am within the final stretch of this race for governor, and **your support could honestly determine its outcome.**
